title: Galium filicaule
taxonomy: Galium filicaule is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which consists of a diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as coffee or madder family. This classification places Galium filicaule in the order Gentianales, a group that also includes other flowering plant families like Apocynaceae and Loganiaceae. At the kingdom level, Galium filicaule falls under Plantae, the broad category of plant life. The genus Galium is part of the family Rubiaceae, and Galium filicaule is a specific species within this genus. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Rubiaceae family.

side_effects: Galium filicaule is a plant species known for its potential medicinal properties. intro: Galium filicaule, also known as Baldr's Polypody, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, characterized by its creeping habit and distinctive white, fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and other systemic problems. The bioactive constituents of Galium filicaule include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a dried leaf or root infusion, as well as a tincture or decoction, to utilize its therapeutic potential in treating various health conditions.
